descriptions: UWSA and UWSP were founded together as twin organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: ethnic Wa cadres under the command of Bao Youxiang announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

descriptions: UWSA is the military wing of UWSP.
edges.evidence_quote: The United Wa State Army ... is the military wing of the United Wa State Party (UWSP)

descriptions: The PKK cooperated with MKP as part of the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2016, the PKK helped to launch the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other Kurdish and Turkish revolutionary leftist, socialist and communist groups (including the TKP/ML , THKP-C/MLSPB , MKP , TKEP/L , TİKB , DKP , DK and MLKP ) with the aim of overthrowing the Turkish government of Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

descriptions: Quinn's Beach Gang mentored and partnered with the Maceos, who later succeeded Quinn's organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Quinn became a mentor, and then a partner, to Sam and Rosario Maceo , who later took over the Galveston underworld.

descriptions: The Downtown Gang and Beach Gang were rivals, fighting for control of Galveston island.
edges.evidence_quote: They were the rival gang of Ollie Quinn and Dutch Voight's Beach Gang.

descriptions: The Maceo Organization evolved from the Beach Gang, with Quinn and Voight becoming partners.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Beach Gang

descriptions: The infobox lists the Mattei clan as a rival of the Petit Bar Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mattei clan , Movement for Self-Determination (Orsoni clan)

descriptions: Hells Angels founded the Renegades as a puppet gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels founded a puppet gang in Prince George known as the Renegades who in turn founded another puppet gang known as the Crew.

descriptions: Independent Soldiers and Renegades cooperated as Hells Angels flunkies in Prince George.
edges.evidence_quote: The Independent Soldier chapter in Prince George came to dominate the drug trade in that city alongside the Renegades who were also 'Hells Angels flunkies'

descriptions: Renegades founded the Crew as a puppet gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels founded a puppet gang in Prince George known as the Renegades who in turn founded another puppet gang known as the Crew.

VVT.description: Canadian Tamil street gang originating in Toronto, founded in the early 1990s by Sri Lankan Tamil immigrants. Named after the town of Valvettithurai in northern Sri Lanka. Involved in drug trafficking, fraud, weapons trafficking, counterfeiting, and extortion. Engaged in a long-running conflict with the rival AK Aannan gang. Now considered largely inactive.

descriptions: VVT and AK Aannan were engaged in a violent conflict (war) during the mid 1990s to early 2000s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the mid 1990s to early 2000s, the VVT had an ongoing war with another Tamil gang called the AK Aannan based out of Scarborough.

descriptions: VVT and AK Aannan had a brief truce in 1998, which ended after a murder in 1999.
edges.evidence_quote: There was a brief truce in 1998 after community members brokered a peace deal in a Richmond Hill Hindu temple; however, a murder a year later renewed the fighting.

Los Caparrapos.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary group that emerged as dissidents of the Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia/Clan del Golfo. Based in the Bajo Cauca region of Antioquia, they engaged in massacres, murders, extortion, forced displacement, drug trafficking, and illegal mining. The group was dismantled in 2021 after the death of its leader Robinson Gil Tapias.

descriptions: The Clan del Golfo is at war with its dissident group Los Caparrapos.
edges.evidence_quote: una guerra entre el Clan del Golfo y su grupo disidente "Los Caparrapos"

descriptions: Los Caparrapos are dissidents from Clan del Golfo, formed in January 2018, disputing territory.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Caparrapos (grupo formado por 180 disidentes del Clan del Golfo que, debido a los golpes dados a este grupo por parte de las autoridades en la zona, se separaron en enero de 2018 disputándoles el control del territorio en el Bajo Cauca)

descriptions: Bloque Héroes de Castaño (part of Clan del Golfo) is at war with Los Caparrapos.
edges.evidence_quote: Se presume que serían ellos quienes están en guerra contra Los Caparrapos (grupo disidente del Clan del Golfo)

descriptions: Los Caparrapos were part of the AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Este grupo y otros más harían parte de las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC) de Vicente Castaño.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos broke their alliance with Clan del Golfo, leading to war from 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: lo que hizo romper su alianza con el Clan del Golfo desatando una guerra desde 2017

descriptions: Los Caparrapos are dissidents of the Clan del Golfo, indicating a structural break.
edges.evidence_quote: Son disidentes de las Autodefensas Gaitanistas de Colombia y/o Clan del Golfo.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos have alliances with CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Tienen alianzas con el Cartel Jalisco Nueva Generación de México.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos have pacts with Los Pachenca to target Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: ya tiene pactos con "Los Pachenca" para dar de baja a cualquier integrante del Clan del Golfo

descriptions: Survivors of Los Paisas joined Los Caparrapos.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Paisas fueron abatidos y detenidos por la Fuerza Pública, y los sobrevivientes se unieron a Los Caparrapos.

descriptions: Los Caparrapos are possibly allied with ELN against Clan del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: Están posiblemente aliados con el ELN y las disidencias de las FARC-EP en su guerra contra el Clan del Golfo

descriptions: Los Caparrapos sold their franchise to Oficina de Envigado.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Caparrapos habrían vendido presuntamente su franquicia en el Bajo Cauca antioqueño a la Oficina de Envigado.

descriptions: Oficina de Envigado attempted to buy Los Caparrapos to avoid disappearing.
edges.evidence_quote: viéndosen obligados, al parecer, a comprar a Los Caparrapos en el Bajo Cauca para no desaparecer

descriptions: The Rascals is listed as a rival of Toonerville Rifa 13.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Armenian Power , The Avenues , Westside Locos The Rascals, Frogtown Rifa , Vineland Boys , MS-13 , Russian mafia , Norte-14

descriptions: The Mazzei clan attempted a coup against the Santapaola Mafia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: His nephew, Gesualdo La Rocca, supported an attempted coup by the rival Mazzei clan to displace the Santapaola leadership in Catania.

descriptions: The Catania faction of Cursoti is closely linked to the Mazzei clan.
edges.evidence_quote: la seconda, detta dei Cursoti catanesi , era guidata da Giuseppe Garozzo detto Pippo 'u maritatu, saldamente legato al clan storicamente capeggiato da Santo Mazzei detto ' u Carcagnusu .

descriptions: Infobox lists Cartel Gente Nueva De Michoacán as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cartel Gente Nueva De Michoacán

descriptions: The Gianola brothers feuded with the Adamo gang over control of rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: Between 1912 and 1913, the Gianola brothers took over the Wyandotte area rackets gang starting a feud with Wyandotte Mafia boss Vito Adamo.

descriptions: The Gianola brothers and the Vitale gang fought a war for control of Detroit's Italian underworld.
edges.evidence_quote: In late 1918, Vitale broke away from the Gianola gang and established himself independently, aligning himself with the remnants of the Bosco group. What became known as the 'Gianola-Vitale War' emerged as one of the bloodiest battles in the history of the Detroit Mafia.

Gang des Tractions Avant.description: The Gang des Tractions Avant was a criminal gang in the Pigalle quarter of Paris, composed of former Carlingue militia members, lapsed police officers, and criminals from the French Resistance. Active in the mid-20th century, the gang was named after its preferred vehicle, the Citroën 11CV 'Traction'. Its methods influenced later gangs and inspired various cultural works.

descriptions: The Gang des Tractions Avant derived its methods from the Bonnot Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Its methods were largely derived from those of the Bonnot Gang

descriptions: The Gang des Tractions Avant was composed of former members of the Carlingue militia.
edges.evidence_quote: made up of surviving members of the Carlingue militia

descriptions: The methods of the Gang des Tractions Avant were continued by the Gang des Postiches.
edges.evidence_quote: were mostly continued by a number of other gangs, notably the gang des postiches

Mullen Gang.description: The Mullen Gang was an Irish-American street gang operating in South Boston, Massachusetts, founded in the 1950s. Engaged in racketeering, armed robbery, auto theft, and burglary, it fought a decade-long war with the Killeen Gang for control of criminal rackets. In 1972, both gangs were consolidated under the Winter Hill Gang. The gang derived its name from a commemorative plaque to war hero John Joseph Mullen.

descriptions: The Killeen Gang and the Mullen Gang were in conflict in the early 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, another mob war was taking place in South Boston between two other Irish-American gangs: the Killeen Gang... and the Mullen Gang...

descriptions: The Mullen Gang and Killeen Gang fought a gang war for control of South Boston rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: After a decade-long gang war against another Irish mob group, the Killeen Gang, for control of criminal rackets in South Boston, both gangs were consolidated under the control of the Winter Hill Gang in 1972.

descriptions: The Mullen Gang was allied with the Winter Hill Gang, as per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Winter Hill Gang

descriptions: The Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Mullen Gang after the war.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, another mob war was taking place in South Boston between two other Irish-American gangs: the Killeen Gang... and the Mullen Gang...

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Mullen Gang after a gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: absorbing the remnants of the Mullen and Killeen gangs of South Boston

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Mullen Gang after gang wars.
edges.evidence_quote: absorbing the remnants of the Mullen and Killeen gangs of South Boston following a series of Irish gang wars

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang absorbed the remnants of the Mullen Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: then absorbing the remnants of the Mullen and Killeen gangs of South Boston following a series of Irish gang wars

descriptions: Winter Hill Gang absorbed remnants of the Mullen Gang after conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: absorbing the remnants of the Mullen and Killeen gangs of South Boston

descriptions: Lobos members joined Hells Angels in a mass patch-over.
edges.evidence_quote: On 29 December 2000, most of the Ontario biker gangs in a mass 'patch over' ... joined the Hells Angels. ... 179 bikers from the Satan's Choice, Para-Dice Riders, the Last Chance, the Lobos, the Outlaws and the Rock Machine joined the Hells Angels

descriptions: Lobos patched over to the Hells Angels in 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: This resulted in 168 members of the Para-Dice Riders, Satan's Choice, Lobos and Last Chance "patching" to the Angels.

descriptions: Lobos merged into Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: Satan's Choice, along with most of the other major Ontario biker gangs, including the Loners, Lobos, and Para-Dice Riders, 'patched over' to the Hells Angels on December 29, 2000.

descriptions: Satan's Choice allied with Lobos in 1981 to counter the Outlaws.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1981, Satan's Choice made an alliance with the Lobos and the Chosen Few gangs to improve their bargaining power against their rivals, the Outlaws.

descriptions: Satan's Choice worked closely with Lobos to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.
edges.evidence_quote: A report by the police Organized Crime Committee from 1989 stated that Satan's Choice, the Vagabonds and the Lobos were working closely to manufacture and distribute methamphetamine.

descriptions: Srigala Hitam was one of the small units that took allegiance to SP88.
edges.evidence_quote: These small units took up names such as Pasukan Siluman (Ghost Squad), Srigala Hitam (Black Jackal), Pemotong Leher (Neck Cutters) and Garuda Putih (White Eagle).

Venice 13.description: Mexican American street gang based in the Oakwood neighborhood of Venice, Los Angeles, California. Founded in the 1920s or 1930s, it is one of the earliest gangs in the city. Affiliated with Sureños and the Mexican Mafia, it is known for deadly violence and a long-running rivalry with Culver City Boys 13. The gang remains active in drug trafficking and street crime.

descriptions: Venice 13 is listed as a rival of Crips in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Aryan Brotherhood, Bloods, Florencia 13, Latin Kings, People Nation, Playboys, Santa Monica 13, Tiny Rascal Gang, United Blood Nation, Venice 13

descriptions: Culver City Boys are rivals with Venice 13.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street Gang , Venice Shoreline Crips Venice 13

descriptions: Santa Monica 13 and Venice 13 are allies after a truce.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia , Sureños , Venice 13 (after the truce)

descriptions: Venice 13 has rivalry with 18th Street gang.
edges.evidence_quote: As well as 18th Street gang (particularly the 106th St in Inglewood), as well as the Grave Yard Gangsta Crips in Santa Monica and Culver City 13 in the Mar Vista neighborhood.

descriptions: Venice 13 lists 18th Street gang as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 has deadly street violence with Culver City Boys 13.
edges.evidence_quote: It is widely known for its deadly street violence with fellow Mexican-American gang, the Culver City Boys 13.

descriptions: Venice 13 has rivalry with Culver City Boys 13.
edges.evidence_quote: As well as 18th Street gang (particularly the 106th St in Inglewood), as well as the Grave Yard Gangsta Crips in Santa Monica and Culver City 13 in the Mar Vista neighborhood.

descriptions: Venice 13 lists Culver City Boys 13 as a rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Culver City Boys 13

descriptions: Venice 13 has rivalry with Grave Yard Gangsta Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: As well as 18th Street gang (particularly the 106th St in Inglewood), as well as the Grave Yard Gangsta Crips in Santa Monica and Culver City 13 in the Mar Vista neighborhood.

descriptions: Venice 13 lists Lennox13 as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 is affiliated with Mexican Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: It is affiliated with the Sureños and the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: Venice 13 lists PlayBoys Gangster Crips as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 and Santa Monica 13 were rivals but later formed an alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13's main rivals were the Santa Monica 13 and Santa Monica 17th St gangs, however they both settled a truce and formed an alliance.

descriptions: Venice 13 lists Santa Monica 13 as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 and Santa Monica 17th St were rivals but later formed an alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13's main rivals were the Santa Monica 13 and Santa Monica 17th St gangs, however they both settled a truce and formed an alliance.

descriptions: Venice 13 fought gang wars against Venice Shoreline Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: It was amongst the first Mexican gangs to fight gang wars against African-American gangs, especially the local Venice Shoreline Crips which were founded in the mid-1970s.

descriptions: Venice 13 and Culver City Boys 13 united against Venice Shoreline Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: Venice 13 and the Culver City gang went as far as to set aside their own fight to unite against the Shorelines.

descriptions: Venice 13 lists Venice Shoreline Crips as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: Venice 13 and Venice Shoreline Crips agreed to a cease fire in 1994.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1994 Venice 13 and the Shorelines bargained for a cease fire, after a war that left 55 people dead and many more wounded.

descriptions: Venice Shoreline Crips fought with Venice 13 over drug trade control, then agreed to a cease-fire.
edges.evidence_quote: a war broke out between the Shorelines and Venice 13 over control of the Venice drug trade until a cease-fire was arranged between the two gangs

descriptions: Phantom Riders president revived Satan's Choice name; Phantom Riders merged into the new Satan's Choice in 1965.
edges.evidence_quote: Phantom Riders president Bernie Guindon subsequently revived the name and patch of the disbanded club as a way to enrage the Black Diamond Riders.

edges.source: Gang de la Brise de Mer
descriptions: Brise de Mer is suspected of killing members of Armata Corsa.
edges.evidence_quote: The "Brise de Mer" is also suspected of involvement in the 2001 murders of members of Armata Corsa , a separatist Corsican armed group.

Lucchese crime family Brooklyn faction.description: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family that control organized crime activities in the New York metropolitan area, predominantly based in Brooklyn and Staten Island. It was created in the early 2000s by merging the Vario Crew and the 19th Hole Crew. The faction is involved in racketeering, bookmaking, loansharking, extortion, gambling, and other illegal activities. It remains active as part of the Lucchese family.

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Bonanno crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Colombo crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Gambino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is a part of the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family

descriptions: The Vario Crew is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The crew was controlled by Caporegime Paul Vario during the early 1960s into the early 1980s

descriptions: The Bensonhurst Crew is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The 19th Hole Crew , sometimes called Bensonhurst Crew , was based in Bensonhurst, Brooklyn , before it was merged into the Brooklyn faction.

descriptions: The Brooklyn faction is a sub-unit of the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family

edges.target: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: A member transferred between the Brooklyn faction and the New Jersey faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Joseph M. 'Little Joe' Perna ... former member of the New Jersey faction, before joining the Brooklyn faction ... Transferred back to the New Jersey faction

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction reported to a capo who also controlled the Vario-Cutaia Crew, indicating structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: the crew reported to Caporegime Domenico Cutaia who also controlled the Vario-Cutaia Crew

edges.source: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: A former leader of the New Jersey faction became captain of the Brooklyn Faction, indicating structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: George 'Georgie Neck' Zappola – stepped down and became captain of the Brooklyn Faction

descriptions: The Robert's Lounge Crew cooperated with the Vario Crew through the friendship between Burke and Vario.
edges.evidence_quote: Burke led his semi-independent Robert's Lounge Crew , from his bar Robert's Lounge . The Robert's Lounge crew was composed of numerous people who were involved in armed robbery, hijacking, and murder. Although Burke's crew was independent, many members were associates of the Lucchese family through Burke's and Vario's longtime friendship.

descriptions: Mkhedrioni opposed the Bagramyan Battalion.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Gamsakhurdia's government in exile , Abkhazia , Bagramyan Battalion , Confederation of Mountain Peoples of the Caucasus

edges.target: Mo dumplings gang of British Columbia
descriptions: The United Nations lists the Mo dumplings gang as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mo dumplings gang of British Columbia

Aizukotetsu-kai.description: The Aizukotetsu-kai is a yakuza organization based in Kyoto, Japan, founded in 1868 by Senkichi Kousaka. It was one of the first syndicates named under Japan's anti-boryokudan legislation in 1992. At its peak in 1993, it had 1,600 members, but current membership is around 40. In 2005, it allied with the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: The Aizukotetsu-kai allied with the Yamaguchi-gumi in 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2005, the group allied with the Sixth Yamaguchi-gumi, Japan's largest yakuza clan now led by Kenichi Shinoda (Oyabun) and his second-in-command (Wakagashira) Kiyoshi Takayama.

descriptions: Aizukotetsu-kai is an ally of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Inagawa-kai, Soai-kai, Matsuba-kai, Kyōsei-kai, Aizukotetsu-kai, Toa-kai, Fukuhaku-kai, Gōda-ikka, Shinwa-kai

Secondigliano Alliance.description: The Secondigliano Alliance is a strategic alliance of Camorra clans based in Naples, Italy, founded in the late 1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, Edoardo Contini, and Francesco Mallardo. It controls drug trafficking and extortion rackets in Naples and its hinterland, with branches in the Netherlands and Spain. Considered one of the two most powerful Camorra organizations, it has been led by Maria Licciardi and later Vincenzo Licciardi. Despite police crackdowns, it remains active.

descriptions: The Aprea-Cuccaro clan made an agreement with the Secondigliano Alliance since the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: According to investigators, since the 1990s, the Aprea-Cuccaro clan has entered into an agreement with the Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: Bosti is one of the families forming the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: un cartello formato dalle famiglie Licciardi, Contini, Bosti e Mallardo

descriptions: The Secondigliano Alliance is a powerful group within the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: the most powerful groups of the Camorra in the present day are the Mazzarella clan and the Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Secondigliano Alliance is one of the most powerful clans within the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: According to numerous 2019 reports, the most powerful clans of the Camorra are the Secondigliano Alliance and the Mazzarella clan.

descriptions: The De Luca Bossa clan allied with the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In the years when Teresa De Luca Bossa was in command, the clan also made an alliance with the powerful Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: Teresa De Luca Bossa was a member of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: membro di quel cartello conosciuto come Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: The Casamonica clan has ties with the Alleanza di Secondigliano through Salvatore Esposito.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore Esposito racconta un altro rapporto di livello della casata. Esposito, infatti, è figlio di Luigi, detto "Nacchella", braccio destro del defunto Gennaro Licciardi, detto "'a scimmia", uno dei re del narcotraffico, esponente di vertice dell'Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: The Di Biasi had friendships with bosses of the Alleanza di Secondigliano, but these did not provide sufficient protection.
edges.evidence_quote: mal protetto dalle sue amicizie con i boss dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Clan Di Lauro strengthened its alliances with the other clans of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: la cosca rafforzò le sue alleanze con gli altri clan dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: The Di Lauro family acted as external guarantors for the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: con gli stessi Di Lauro come garanti esterni

descriptions: Di Lauro clan strengthened connections within the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Their connections with other clans were strengthened in the Secondigliano Alliance.

descriptions: The Giuliano clan and the Alleanza di Secondigliano fought a violent clash in 1990.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Giuliano e l'Alleanza di Secondigliano : violento scontro avvenuto tra i due potenti gruppi nel 1990

descriptions: Clan Licciardi is a member of the Alleanza di Secondigliano cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan fa parte insieme alle famiglie Contini, Mallardo, Lo Russo, Ferone, Stabile, Prestieri, Bosti e Bocchetti del cartello camorristico denominato Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Gennaro Licciardi founded the Secondigliano Alliance, of which the Licciardi clan is a member.
edges.evidence_quote: He also became the founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance, a coalition of powerful Camorra clans which controls drug trafficking and extortion rackets in many suburbs of Naples.

descriptions: Licciardi is part of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan Contini fa parte dell’«Alleanza di Secondigliano», insieme a clan Mallardo e clan Licciardi.

descriptions: Clan Marfella was supported by the Alleanza di Secondigliano in its conflict with Clan Lago.
edges.evidence_quote: spalleggiato anche dall' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Clan Mariano was at war with Alleanza di Secondigliano in the early 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan dei Mariano fu in guerra prima con i Faiano (i Di Biasi) e poi con l' Alleanza di Secondigliano agli inizi degli anni novanta per il controllo dei Quartieri Spagnoli.

descriptions: Mazzarella was at war with Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: la famiglia Mazzarella entrò in guerra con l'Alleanza di Secondigliano, in particolare contro la famiglia Contini del Vasto-Arenaccia

descriptions: Mazzarella clan opposed the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s the Mazzarellas formed a strong alliance with the Misso and Sarno clans, called Misso-Mazzarella-Sarno, to oppose to the rising power of the Secondigliano Alliance, headed by the Licciardi, Mallardo and Contini bosses.

descriptions: Mazzarella and Sarno clans had clear reasons of hatred towards the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: che avevano chiare ragioni di odio nei confronti dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Misso fought against scissionists close to the Secondigliano clans.
edges.evidence_quote: un conflitto tra il clan Misso del Rione Sanità e alcuni scissionisti guidati da Salvatore Torino, vicino ai clan di Secondigliano

descriptions: The Misso clan and the Alleanza di Secondigliano were in a faida that escalated after 1992.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Misso e l'Alleanza di Secondigliano : faida portata avanti dal boss Giuseppe Misso e dai vertici dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Gaetano Bocchetti, a namesake of the clan, was a proponent of the Alleanza di Secondigliano, indicating a cooperative tie.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan prende il nome da Gennaro Sacco e Gaetano Bocchetti , quest'ultimo fautore dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano .

descriptions: Mazzarella and Sarno clans had clear reasons of hatred towards the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: che avevano chiare ragioni di odio nei confronti dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Clan Sarno was at war with the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: entrò prima in guerra con i clan dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: Stabile was previously allied with the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: in precedenza alleati sotto la bandiera dell' Alleanza di Secondigliano

descriptions: The Commisso 'ndrina is allied with the Secondigliano Alliance in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Commissos are known to have links with the Secondigliano Alliance , considered by the authorities the most powerful Camorra group that is still active. Both organizations are allied in the trafficking and sale of cocaine and marijuana from South America, via the Netherlands.

descriptions: Commisso has close ties with Alleanza di Secondigliano for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Secondo gli inquirenti, la 'ndrina ha stretti rapporti con l' Alleanza di Secondigliano , un potente cartello della camorra .

descriptions: Contini clan is a founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Contini clan is also one of the founding clans of the Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Contini is part of the Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: Il clan Contini fa parte dell’«Alleanza di Secondigliano», insieme a clan Mallardo e clan Licciardi.

descriptions: Lo Russo clan joined the Secondigliano Alliance after the NF dissolved.
edges.evidence_quote: After the end of the NF alliance, the Lo Russo clan joined the newly formed Secondigliano Alliance

descriptions: Lo Russo was previously allied with the Alleanza di Secondigliano.

Baltimore Crew.description: The Baltimore Crew was an Italian-American organized crime group based in Baltimore, Maryland, active from the 1920s to the 1990s. Originally independent, it became a faction of the Gambino crime family in 1955. The group was involved in gambling, extortion, loan sharking, drug trafficking, and murder. It was led by the Corbi family for much of its existence.

descriptions: The Corbi family functionally headed the Baltimore Crew after 1920.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corbi family acquiesced to the Gambino relationship, but maintained local leaders, simply answering to and accessing support from Morici and his New York Gambino connections.

descriptions: The D'Urso family originally led the Baltimore Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: It was originally an independent organization, led by the D'Urso family until the Corbi takeover in the 1920s.

descriptions: The Baltimore Crew became a faction of the Gambino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Baltimore Crew was an Italian-American organized-crime group that ultimately became a faction of the Gambino crime family

descriptions: De Stefano allied with Tegano through marriage during second war.
edges.evidence_quote: the wedding sealed the alliance between the De Stefanos and the powerful Tegano clan.

descriptions: Tempel ov Blood infiltrated and took over Atomwaffen Division.
edges.evidence_quote: The ToB infiltrated and effectively took over the AWD, beginning in 2017.

descriptions: ITS leader claimed influence from Tempel ov Blood.
edges.evidence_quote: A leader of the ITS claimed to have been influenced by the satanic fascist group the Tempel ov Blood .

descriptions: The 107th Street gang operated under the protection of the Reina family, which later became the Lucchese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the gang stole wallets, burglarized stores, and engaged in other illegal activities. The 107th Street gang operated under the protection of Bronx–East Harlem family boss Gaetano "Tom" Reina .

descriptions: Klutas, leader of the College Kidnappers, had connections with John Dillinger's gang.

descriptions: Herrera Organization transported cocaine on behalf of Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 2000s and possibly still today, it is believed that a group known as the Herrera Organization would transport multi-ton quantities of cocaine from South America to Guatemala on behalf of the Sinaloa Cartel.

Stopwatch Gang.description: The Stopwatch Gang was a group of three Canadian bank robbers active from 1974 to 1980. They robbed over 140 banks in the United States and Canada, stealing approximately $15 million Canadian dollars. Known for meticulous planning and non-violent methods, they completed robberies in under two minutes. The gang was named by the FBI after a member wore a stopwatch during a heist.

Família Terror do Amapá.description: Brazilian criminal organization founded in 2015 at the Amapá Penitentiary Administration Institute in Macapá. It operates primarily in Amapá state and has expanded to Pará, Mato Grosso do Sul, Rondônia, and French Guiana. Engaged in drug and arms trafficking, homicides, and robberies. Allied with PCC, TCP, and CCA, and rivals with Comando Vermelho and UCA.

Trinitarios.description: Dominican American criminal organization founded in 1993 on Rikers Island, New York City, by Leonides Sierra and Julio Marine. Initially formed to protect Dominican and Hispanic inmates, it expanded into drug trafficking, murder, and robbery across the northeastern US and internationally. Despite major prosecutions in 2009-2014, the group experienced a resurgence with internal factional violence.

edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: People Nation , Norteños (pandilla) , Pirus , United Blood Nation , Juggalos , Trinitarios (organización criminal)

Venice Shoreline Crips.description: The Venice Shoreline Crips is a set of the Crips gang alliance based in Venice, Los Angeles, California. Formed in the late 1960s in the Oakwood neighborhood, it became heavily involved in narcotics trafficking during the 1980s crack epidemic. The gang has been engaged in violent conflicts with rival gangs such as Culver City 13 and Venice 13. Despite law enforcement efforts, it maintains control over its territory with hundreds of members.
